decks.notes: The deck focuses on ramping up mana with the UrzaTron lands to quickly play powerful Eldrazi creatures and artifacts. Control elements like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon are used to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Kozilek's Command provide hand disruption and removal. The deck also includes some colorless and nonbasic lands to support the various spells and abilities. The overall strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with big threats while controlling the game with powerful spells and abilities.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping mana to cast Primeval Titan and utilize lands like Urza's Saga and Lotus Field to generate additional resources. The key strategy is to tutor for and play Amulet of Vigor to untap lands and generate even more mana, allowing for explosive plays and quickly assembling the Amulet Titan combo for a powerful finish. The deck also includes various utility lands and spells to support the combo and protect against disruption from opponents.

The deck focuses on a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing a mix of creatures with powerful abilities and spells to control the board and deal damage quickly to the opponent. By using efficient removal spells and creatures with haste and evasion, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. Additionally, the deck includes cards that provide card advantage and card selection to maintain pressure on the opponent and ensure consistency in drawing threats.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying a wide array of artifacts to overwhelm the opponent while disrupting their game plan with various hate cards such as Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps recur valuable artifacts from the graveyard, while Arcbound Ravager can help sacrifice them for added value. The inclusion of Urza's Saga enables the deck to consistently tutor for powerful artifacts like Pithing Needle or Shadowspear. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain control of the board through its artifacts and disruption.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures and then bring them back onto the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent. By using cards like Thoughtseize and Thought Scour to help with card selection and filling the graveyard, along with spells like Persist and Unearth to bring back creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones, this deck aims to create a powerful combo of reanimator creatures that can dominate the game. The inclusion of disruptive cards like Spell Pierce and Fatal Push also allows for control over the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this control UrzaTron deck is to assemble the powerful Urza lands (Urza's Mine, Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant) to generate a large amount of mana and cast devastating Eldrazi creatures like Thought-Knot Seer and Emrakul, the Promised End. The deck also utilizes cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Trinisphere and Glaring Fleshraker help to slow down the opponent while Expedition Map and Mind Stone provide consistency and mana acceleration.

This deck aims to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It leverages cards like Terminus and Brainsurge to deal with threats, while using cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler for card advantage and to control the opponent's spells. The deck also includes powerful cards like Isochron Scepter to lock down the opponent's spells, and Consult the Star Charts for card selection. Overall, the focus is on controlling the board and outvaluing the opponent to secure victory.

The deck focuses on assembling a diverse array of creature cards with various abilities that work together to create powerful interactions and combos. It utilizes cards like Agatha's Soul Cauldron, Malevolent Rumble, and Yawgmoth, Thran Physician to manipulate the board state and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The toolbox approach allows the player to search for specific creatures that are needed at any given moment, while also providing flexibility in adapting to different matchups. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its synergistic creature combinations and resource management.

The deck is focused on utilizing cards that allow for discarding and manipulating the graveyard to eventually cast Living End, a powerful sorcery that brings back all creatures from the graveyard to the battlefield. By cycling through the deck quickly with cards like Street Wraith and Subtlety, the player can set up for a big turn where they can overwhelm the opponent with a flood of creatures. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Negation to protect the combo and removal options like Endurance to handle threats from the opponent.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through ramping into powerful Eldrazi creatures. By using cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Kozilek's Command, the deck can disrupt the opponent's game plan while accelerating its own mana production. The Eldrazi creatures like Sire of Seven Deaths and Emrakul, the Promised End provide powerful threats that can quickly close out the game. The deck also includes land destruction elements with cards like Ghost Quarter and World Breaker to further disrupt the opponent's strategies. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its powerful creatures and controlling elements.

The main concept of this Modern Ruby Storm combo deck is to generate as much mana as possible using cards like Pyretic Ritual and Ruby Medallion, along with card draw from Manamorphose and Glimpse the Impossible. Ral, Monsoon Mage is used as a combo piece to deal damage to the opponent. Past in Flames allows for recursion of spells from the graveyard. The deck aims to chain together spells and rituals to cast powerful turn-ending spells like Wish or March of Reckless Joy to overwhelm the opponent and win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent with counterspells and controlling the board with creatures like Generous Ent and Overlord of the Balemurk. Its main strategy revolves around cycling through cards with Street Wraith and Shardless Agent to quickly fill the graveyard, eventually casting Living End to bring back powerful creatures from the graveyard and overwhelm the opponent. The inclusion of Endurance helps protect against graveyard hate and Otawara, Soaring City provides additional card advantage. The deck aims to efficiently combo off and win the game before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards that allow the player to control the board while setting up for the combo finish with Living End. Cards such as Generous Ent, Force of Negation, and Subtlety help to disrupt the opponent's game plan while creatures like Shardless Agent and Overlord of the Balemurk provide card advantage and board presence. By cycling through cards like Street Wraith and Endurance, the deck aims to fill the graveyard with creatures to be brought back all at once with Living End, overwhelming the opponent and securing victory.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells and removal spells while using card draw and planeswalkers to gain card advantage and outvalue the opponent. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while protecting its own win conditions, such as Teferi, Time Raveler and Narset, Parter of Veils. The deck also utilizes lands that can provide additional value or card selection. Overall, the key concept is to establish and maintain control of the game until the player can successfully close out the game with their powerful cards and abilities.

This deck focuses on using ramp cards to quickly generate mana and cast powerful Eldrazi creatures. It also utilizes control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan, with removal spells like Dismember and Kozilek's Command. The Eldrazi creatures, such as Emrakul, the Promised End and World Breaker, provide powerful threats that can quickly close out the game. The deck aims to establish control over the game by ramping into these big threats while disrupting the opponent's strategy, ultimately overpowering them with its high-impact creatures.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Puresteel Paladin, Colossus Hammer, and Leyline Axe to quickly equip a powerful creature like an Inkmoth Nexus and deal lethal infect damage. The deck includes search and draw cards like Stoneforge Mystic and Steelshaper's Gift to find combo pieces quickly, while also utilizing Urza's Saga to tutor for artifacts. The addition of Mox Opal and Sunbaked Canyon helps with mana acceleration, allowing for a fast and explosive win condition. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with an unexpected and lethal combo attack.
